fields.description:
## Status — RESCOPED + CLOSED (operator-approved 2026-07-18)

This story is closed against a **rescoped** acceptance set. It had two separable halves:

1. **A DETERMINISTIC half — DESIGNED AND BUILT.** The seeded-defect **mutant corpus**, the
   **mutation-assembly + benchmark-driver harness**, the **pure counting helpers**, the
   **pre-registered thresholds**, and their **golden tests**. This lives as WIP in the `cal-e2`
   worktree on branch `calib/6982-e2` (`assemble_e2_mutants.py`, `run_e2_benchmark.py`,
   `e2_counting.py`, `runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl`, `runs/e2_thresholds.json`,
   `tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_counting.py`, `test_e2_benchmark.py`). It is **not landed on
   main** — the harness's only runtime purpose is to drive the rejected batch (below), so landing
   it would ship a moot LLM-spend entry point. It is recorded here as documented WIP.

2. **An EXPENSIVE LLM BATCH BASELINE — PERMANENTLY REJECTED on cost, and SUPERSEDED.** The
   ~100-run live scoring of the mutant/control corpus by the current gate (`run_e2_benchmark.py`'s
   `review_plan` sweep, and the `e2_baseline.json` / `e2_run_validity.json` / `e2_verdicts.jsonl`
   it would emit) is **dropped**. It is superseded by story **d8a5** (change **#798, MERGED**):
   the standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder (`criterion_effectiveness.py`, on main),
   which accumulates the SAME detection / blocking-FP signal from real production reviews at **$0
   marginal LLM cost**. Baseline **mining** from history is also dead: only ~4 `plan-v3` sidecar
   baselines exist against the ~200 needed — the `impact_model_version` bump stranded the history.

The design record below (Why / classes / mechanism) is retained as the account of what was built;
the Acceptance Criteria are rescoped to the delivered deterministic WIP only.

## Why

R1 (asserted-capability probe), R3 (decomposition-shape), and R4 (necessity gate) all gate on
DETECTION POWER, which can only be measured against plans whose defects are KNOWN. This story
builds that ground truth by CONSTRUCTION: take real cleanly-closed plans, seed exactly one defect
per class, and the mutation itself is the label — no fresh LLM adjudication (which would duplicate
the gate's own sidecar and be circular; that is why E1's finding-level LLM labeling was
invalidated). The unmutated originals are the controls that bound blocking false positives.

Named verbatim in `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` §6 R3: "Seeded-flaw eval: take
10 real historical epics that closed cleanly; generate mutated variants ... Pass condition: ≥7/10
mutants flagged, ≤1/10 originals flagged." E2 generalizes that to all five defect classes.

**Five defect classes** (each mutated mechanically from a real cleanly-closed base plan):

1. **absent asserted capability** — plan asserts a named module provides a capability it lacks.
2. **layer-cake split** — children re-partitioned by architectural layer so no single child
   yields an end-to-end observable outcome.
3. **consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge** — a child names an artifact a sibling creates with
   no `depends_on`/`blocks` edge.
4. **unnecessary mechanism** — one over-action line injects a NAMED mechanism the plan does not
   need (FixedBench over-action), selected deterministically by `hash(base_id) % len(templates)`.
5. **untagged operational AC** — mutation strips the `[operator-attested]` tag from an AC whose
   completion evidence lives outside the codebase.

The corpus was built from `runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl` under a codebase-grounded "cleanly-closed"
filter (`post_claim_edit_class=="none"` AND `reopen_count==0` AND `force_close==false` AND live
`closed` status AND, for container classes 2 & 3, ≥2 children). Counting rule pinned in the pure,
golden-tested `e2_counting.py` (`classify_pair`, `run_validity`) which handles the gate's
PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE verdicts and the ≥0.20 INDETERMINATE run-validity cap. Store isolation is
by construction: the harness runs the gate against a standalone `git clone --local --branch tickets`
of the store (its own `.git` directory), so mutation commits never touch the real `.tickets-tracker`.

**Why the batch was never worth its cost.** The corpus + harness + counting helpers are complete
and construct-valid, but the ONLY thing running them adds is a one-shot snapshot of today's gate's
detection/FP numbers — which d8a5's standing recorder now produces continuously from real reviews
for free, and which mining cannot reconstruct (stranded history). The operator therefore rejected
the ~100-run batch permanently and accepted the deterministic build as the deliverable.

## Scope (delivered as WIP on `calib/6982-e2`; NOT landed on main)

- `harnesses/assemble_e2_mutants.py` — mutation-assembly script (deterministic, no LLM).
- `harnesses/e2_counting.py` — pure `classify_pair` / `run_validity` counting helpers (no LLM).
- `harnesses/run_e2_benchmark.py` — benchmark driver. Its store-clone/isolation and `emit_baseline`
  run-validity-guard SEAMS are built and unit-tested offline; its live `review_plan` sweep is the
  rejected batch and is NOT to be executed.
- `runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl`, `runs/e2_thresholds.json` — corpus + pre-registered thresholds.
- `tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_counting.py`, `test_e2_benchmark.py` — golden/synthetic tests
  (offline; no LLM).
- README section documenting the design as WIP + the batch deferral.
- NO `src/rebar` gate/source changes (E2 was measurement, not a gate change).

## Acceptance Criteria (rescoped — delivered deterministic WIP only)

- [ ] **Mutant corpus BUILT.** `runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl` holds ≥10 mutant rows for each of
  defect classes 1–4 (class 5 degrades to N≥5 with the shortfall recorded per the class-5 ladder),
  each paired with exactly one control row; every row carries `pair_id`, `defect_class`, `role`,
  `source_ticket_id`, `plan_text`, `children`, and (mutants) `mutation_op` + `mutation_diff`; each
  class-5 control's `plan_text` retains an `[operator-attested]` AC line the mutant strips. Built
  and verifiable in the `calib/6982-e2` worktree; deterministic (no LLM).
- [ ] **Thresholds pre-registered.** `runs/e2_thresholds.json` pins per-class detection /
  blocking-FP targets for all 5 classes plus the INDETERMINATE run-validity cap. Built as WIP;
  deterministic.
- [ ] **Counting helpers BUILT + golden-tested.** `e2_counting.py`'s pure `classify_pair`
  (INDETERMINATE→FN not FP; multi-criteria set-intersection attribution) and `run_validity`
  (≥0.20 INDETERMINATE-cap) pass `pytest tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_counting.py` offline (no
  LLM). Built as WIP on `calib/6982-e2`.
- [ ] **Baseline-emit guard BUILT + tested.** `run_e2_benchmark.emit_baseline` enforces (not merely
  computes) the run-validity guard — returns non-zero and writes no `out_path` when any class
  exceeds the INDETERMINATE cap — proven by the synthetic-fixture integration test
  `pytest tests/unit/experiments/test_e2_benchmark.py` (offline; imports the seam only, no LLM).
  Built as WIP.
- [ ] **Store-isolation implemented BY CONSTRUCTION.** The harness sources its mutant store as a
  standalone `git clone --local --branch tickets` (own `.git` DIRECTORY, not a linked-worktree
  pointer) and asserts standalone-ness before any injection, so the real `.tickets-tracker` cannot
  be contaminated. Implemented in `run_e2_benchmark.py`; verified by code inspection (the
  live `--self-check-isolation` run is part of the deferred batch and is NOT executed).
- [ ] **[operator-attested] Batch baseline DEFERRED — permanently rejected on cost, superseded by
  d8a5.** The live ~100-run `run_e2_benchmark.py` gate sweep and its `e2_baseline.json` /
  `e2_run_validity.json` / `e2_verdicts.jsonl` outputs are intentionally NOT produced. The
  detection / blocking-FP signal they would have measured is now accumulated at $0 marginal LLM
  cost by d8a5's standing `criterion_effectiveness.py` recorder (change #798, MERGED, on main), and
  history-mining is dead (~4 `plan-v3` baselines vs ~200 needed). Evidence lives outside this
  codebase: operator decision 2026-07-18 + d8a5 / change #798. This AC is closed by operator
  attestation, not machine check.
- [ ] **Design documented as WIP.** The README section records the seeded-defect design, the
  counting/run-validity rules, the store-isolation mechanism, and the batch deferral +
  d8a5-supersession, so the WIP status and the drop rationale are auditable.
